A Boeing (McDonnell-Douglas) MD-11F.
Chihoon Shin (listed in the report as 'Shin, Chihoon').
About 1713:30 EST.

Emergency AD 2025-23-53 (issued November 14, 2025) superseded Emergency AD 2025-23-51 (issued November 8, 2025). The superseding AD also expanded scope to include MD-11/MD-11F and DC-10 series airplanes.
10 miles.
KSDF, 501 ft msl.
IFR.
UNITED PARCEL SERVICE CO.
14 total fatalities (3 crewmembers aboard the airplane and 11 people on the ground).
The relief officer, about 15,250 total hours.
The first officer, with about 994 hours in the MD-11 make and model.

No, the SDI was not overdue. The SDI of the left pylon aft mount lugs would have been due at 29,200 cycles, and the accident airplane had only 21,043 cycles. The report notes the task 'had not been accomplished' because it was not yet due.
American Airlines flight 191 had more fatalities. AA191 had 273 total fatalities (271 on board plus 2 on the ground); UPS flight 2976 had 14 total fatalities. The difference is 259.
No. An NTSB ATC specialist was on site to collect ATC information, but an ATC group was not formed.
No. The forward lug's outboard fracture surface consisted entirely of overstress with no indications of fatigue cracking.

No. The right engine remained attached to the right wing pylon; both were found at the accident site. The right pylon-to-wing forward and aft mounts had separated from the right wing pylon (and were found about 87 ft from the right engine), but the right engine itself stayed on the pylon.

No. This is a preliminary report and is explicitly marked as preliminary and subject to change. It describes findings (e.g., fatigue and overstress fractures at the left pylon aft mount lugs) but does not state a probable cause. The investigation is ongoing.
GE CF-6 engines. The MD-11 can be equipped with either GE CF-6 series or Pratt & Whitney PW4000 series engines; the accident airplane was equipped with GE CF-6 engines.
